content.text:
```json
{
  "sources": [
    {
      "title": "Beethoven's Piano Sonatas: A Short Companion",
      "author": "Charles Rosen",
      "year": 2002,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "Yale University Press",
      "url": "https://yalebooks.yale.edu/book/9780300090703/beethovens-piano-sonatas/",
      "description": "Rosen provides a rigorous analytical account of Op. 13, examining the structural role of the slow introduction and its cyclical recurrence within the first movement, the harmonic language of the exposition, and Beethoven's dramatic use of the Grave material as an interpolation — arguing for its unprecedented formal innovation within the Classical sonata tradition."
    },
    {
      "title": "Beethoven and the Construction of Genius: Musical Politics in Vienna, 1792–1803",
      "author": "Tia DeNora",
      "year": 1995,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "University of California Press",
      "url": "https://www.ucpress.edu/book/9780520215436/beethoven-and-the-construction-of-genius",
      "description": "While contextualizing Beethoven's early Vienna career, DeNora offers analytical commentary on Op. 13's reception and its role in establishing Beethoven's public persona, noting how the first movement's rhetorical intensity and the published title 'Grande Sonate pathétique' were instrumentalized in his self-promotion among Viennese aristocratic patrons."
    },
    {
      "title": "The Classical Style: Haydn, Mozart, Beethoven",
      "author": "Charles Rosen",
      "year": 1971,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "W. W. Norton & Company",
      "url": "https://wwnorton.com/books/9780393040203",
      "description": "In this landmark study, Rosen analyzes the Op. 13 first movement as a defining example of Beethoven's expansion of Classical sonata form, specifically addressing the dramatic weight of the Grave introduction, the tonal tension of the Allegro di molto exposition, and how Beethoven integrates the introductory material into the recapitulation in a manner that disrupts conventional formal expectations."
    }
  ]
}
```
